title: Nuclei-seq Protocol
author: Mandy Xu
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8b8hsrw

steps.instruction: Rack 4 falcon tubes (lysis buff, wash buff, nuclei, intermediate) and 1 FACS tube on ice

Chill centrifuges (4°C 800 g 10 min), clean hood, fill Styrofoam boxes: one with dry ice (petri dish and razor blade); one with ice packed and slanted for petri dish away from the user; one with ice with falcon tubes (above), 1 weighed epp tube and douncer with A pestle; Clean douncer, pestle and forceps with ethanol, wrap forceps and ethanol again; Pestle B and forceps on kimwipe beside ice in hood; Ranin wide bore tips and p1000;
Prep 5 epi tubes on a rack by scope: Three for checking the completion of lysis; One for final resuspension for FACs " leave on ice; One for counting.
Weigh tissue (30-50mg ideal, save rest) and cut tissue into 1-2mm3 using a chilled razor on dry ice
Add lysis buffer (enough to cover) and transfer to the ice to continue chopping, using a pipette to rinse off blade using the lysis buffer of the sample
Transfer to nuclei tube and lysis for 5 min
Dounce supernatant first (1-2 strokes) and transfer to 4th tube
Dounce pellet in thirds (2 strokes)
Add enough lysis buffer to resuspend pellet in douncer, dounce and transfer to 4th tube
Douncing is done when solution appears milky
Resuspend 4th tube with wide bore tip and check with residual on tip and tip of p20 in SYBR using countess chips
Transfer super to nuclei tube, leaving behind thepellet
Transfer pellet back to douncer and dounce with pestle B (1/3-1/2 of pellet depending on toughness) " 2-4 strokes
Rinse out douncer with lysis buffer
Transfer to nuclei tube
Resuspend nuclei and count again with leftover in p1000 tip " dounce again based on results
Spin at 800g for 10 min
Wash with 2ml of wash buffer
Spin at 800g for 10 min
Wash again with 2ml of wash buffer
Spin for 10 min at 800g and resuspend in 1ml
Filter with 40um Flowmi cell strainer, transfer to 1.5ml LoBind tube on ice
Count nucleiSpin at 800g for 10 min
Wash again with 2ml of wash buffer
Spin for 10 min at 800g and resuspend in 1ml
Filter with 40um Flowmi cell strainer, transfer to 1.5ml LoBind tube on ice
Count nuclei
40ul buff + 5ul nuclei If >1 million use 10ul of DAPI If <1 million use 6-8ul of DAPI DAPI requires 5 min incubation
Make note of nuclei condition, number of strokes, nuclei size etc after loading
DO NOT DISCARD THE WASH AND RESUSPENSION BUFFER, KEEP IT ON ICE.
After FACS, Spin at 800g for 10 min, resuspend the pellet in cold wash and resuspension buffer (volume calculated so that the final conc is around 1000nuclei/ul)
Count and take images
Make note of nuclei condition, number of strokes, nuclei size etc after loading
Stain the nuclei with DAPI at the concentration suggested by the manufacturer.
Cell sorter: Influx BRV (TMDT, LAB 2-504), sorting for 1-1.5hrs.
Gating: DAPI positive, and exclude any debris/ nuclei aggregates within the DAPI positive gating.
Collect nuclei in the Wash and Resuspension Buffer.
Wash the nuclei with the Wash and Resuspension Buffer.
Count the nuclei and proceed to 10X 3’ RNA seq directly.

After FACS, Spin at 800g for 10 min, remove the supernatant (sheath buffer+ wash and resuspension buffer), resuspend the pellet in a fresh, cold wash and resuspension buffer (volume calculated so that the final conc is around 1000nuclei/ul)Stain the nuclei with DAPI at concentration suggested by the manufacturer.
